label: Bayesian & approximate inference

node.label: Bayesian & approximate inference
node.definition: Posterior beliefs over hidden variables or parameters are estimated exactly or approximately.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Bayesian & approximate inference is modeled as depending on Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design and feeding downstream nodes such as MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing, k-NN & Naive Bayes, Gaussian processes.
examples: atlas downstream link to MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing
atlas downstream link to k-NN & Naive Bayes
atlas downstream link to Gaussian processes
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Bayesian & approximate inference has a `uses` dependency on Probability theory because Probability theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Bayesian & approximate inference has a `uses` dependency on Statistics & experimental design because Statistics & experimental design is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Bayesian & approximate inference has a `uses` dependency on Numerical analysis because Numerical analysis is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
